mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly AI interface designed for self-hosting various AI models, including Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It is ideal for users looking to deploy AI solutions offline with a focus on extensibility and user management. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and potential complexity in setup compared to managed solutions.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Open WebUI Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Open WebUI easily.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

```

> This command adds the official Open WebUI Helm repository.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
This command installs Open WebUI using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --namespace open-webui --create-namespace --version 0.8.8

```

> This installs Open WebUI in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Create Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for Open WebUI, which defines the configuration for your AI deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: openwebui.io/v1
kind: WebUI
metadata:
  name: my-webui
spec:
  apiUrl: 'https://api.openai.com'
  model: 'gpt-3.5-turbo'
EOF

```

> Replace the apiUrl and model with your desired configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: openwebui.io/v1
kind: WebUI
metadata:
  name: my-webui
spec:
  apiUrl: 'https://api.openai.com'
  model: 'gpt-3.5-turbo'
EOF

steps.title: Verify Open WebUI Installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Open WebUI deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Look for the pod status to be 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Open WebUI deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss for any configurations or user data stored in the deployment.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step will upgrade Open WebUI to the latest version while ensuring minimal downtime.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n open-webui -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --namespace open-webui --version 0.8.8

kubectl uncordon $(kubectl get nodes -o name)

```

> Drains the pods to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades Open WebUI to the specified version.
> This command uncordons the nodes after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n open-webui -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n open-webui' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n open-webui'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Resource quota exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The namespace has hit its resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review and adjust the resource quotas or limits set for the namespace.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Ollama models, improved API integration capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Open WebUI interface accessible via the specified service URL, with all components running smoothly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
